introSection: Batman – The Dark Knight is a Bolliger & Mabillard floorless roller coaster located at Six Flags New England in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA. The ride was installed by Martin & Vleminckx and opened in 2002.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Construction of the attraction began in September 2001. The ride's name was announced in February 2002.

Batman – The Dark Knight officially opened on April 20, 2002. It was the second Batman-themed Bolliger & Mabillard floorless coaster to be constructed by Six Flags, with the first one being Batman Knight Flight at Six Flags Worlds of Adventure.

The ride was briefly renamed Batman: The Ride in 2008 due to a proposed new indoor Wild Mouse roller coaster by Mack Rides, called The Dark Knight Coaster. This new ride was then cancelled in April and the name was reverted to the original. However, The Dark Knight Coaster would open the following year at Six Flags Mexico.

In 2022, a single rider line was added to the ride.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride features a twister layout. A banked curve goes through the vertical loop, the zero-g roll crosses through the dive loop and the corkscrews are interlocking. The ride also features a pre-drop, typical of Bolliger & Mabillard roller coasters.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 2 trains with 7 cars per train. Riders are arranged 4 across in a single row for a total of 28 riders per train.

fullText:
Batman – The Dark Knight is a Bolliger & Mabillard floorless roller coaster located at Six Flags New England in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA. The ride was installed by Martin & Vleminckx and opened in 2002.

Construction of the attraction began in September 2001. The ride's name was announced in February 2002.

Batman – The Dark Knight officially opened on April 20, 2002. It was the second Batman-themed Bolliger & Mabillard floorless coaster to be constructed by Six Flags, with the first one being Batman Knight Flight at Six Flags Worlds of Adventure.

The ride was briefly renamed Batman: The Ride in 2008 due to a proposed new indoor Wild Mouse roller coaster by Mack Rides, called The Dark Knight Coaster. This new ride was then cancelled in April and the name was reverted to the original. However, The Dark Knight Coaster would open the following year at Six Flags Mexico.

In 2022, a single rider line was added to the ride.

The ride features a twister layout. A banked curve goes through the vertical loop, the zero-g roll crosses through the dive loop and the corkscrews are interlocking. The ride also features a pre-drop, typical of Bolliger & Mabillard roller coasters.

2 trains with 7 cars per train. Riders are arranged 4 across in a single row for a total of 28 riders per train.
